diagrams.title: E-Waste Composition Breakdown
diagrams.description: Pie chart showing typical material composition of e-waste by weight percentage
diagrams.purpose: Visualize material distribution to understand recycling potential and hazard priorities

diagrams.title: E-Waste Generation Sources
diagrams.description: Flow diagram showing three main sources feeding into total e-waste stream
elements: Households (40%): Mobile phones, PCs, TVs, appliances
Business/Industry (35%): Office equipment, servers, industrial electronics
diagrams.purpose: Understand generation patterns to design targeted collection and awareness programs

diagrams.title: Material Value Pyramid
diagrams.description: Pyramid showing relationship between material quantity and economic value in e-waste
elements: Top (1%): Gold, Silver, Palladium - Extremely high value
Upper Middle (4%): Copper, Aluminum - High value
Base (80%): Plastics, Glass, Iron - Low value but bulk weight
diagrams.purpose: Illustrate why precious metal recovery is economically crucial despite small quantities
